req.title: Vital signs validation (negative)
req.statement: An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in clinically plausible range; out-of-range shall be flagged for review. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
expected_outcomes: flag raised if out of range

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers vital-signs Observation validation with in-range acceptance, out-of-range flagging, and negative paths. I selected: (1) EP positive – a normal heart-rate value inside the plausible range to confirm storage without a flag; (2) NEG – POST without a mandatory 'subject' element to confirm 422 rejection; (3) BVA boundary – a heart-rate value exactly at the upper clinical boundary (100 bpm) to verify flag behaviour at the edge; (4) DT – conflicting status transition (final→entered-in-error without required fields) to cover decision-table rules; (5) STC – state transition from 'preliminary' to 'final' for a valid out-of-range value to verify flag is raised and persisted through the status change. Each test uses a 'clinician' auth fixture and a shared FHIR-Lite base-URL fixture, keeping payloads inline and assertions tight.

tests.name: test_vitals_normal_heartrate_stored_no_flag
preconditions: FHIR-Lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /Observation with category vital-signs, code 8867-4 (heart rate), valueQuantity 72 bpm
expected: Response body does NOT contain a 'review-flag' extension or extension list is absent
trace: R-HLT-069: no flag raised for in-range value
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vitals_normal_heartrate_stored_no_flag(base_url, clinician_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P-001"},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    extensions = body.get("extension", [])
    flag_urls = [e.get("url", "") for e in extensions]
    assert not any("review-flag" in u for u in flag_urls)

tests.name: test_vitals_missing_subject_rejected
preconditions: FHIR-Lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /Observation with category vital-signs but 'subject' element omitted
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue severity 'error'
trace: R-HLT-069: missing mandatory elements error pathway
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vitals_missing_subject_rejected(base_url, clinician_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues)
    diagnostics = " ".join(i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in issues)
    assert "subject" in diagnostics.lower()

tests.name: test_vitals_heartrate_at_upper_boundary_flagged
preconditions: FHIR-Lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Clinical upper boundary for resting heart rate is defined as 100 bpm
actions: POST /Observation with category vital-signs, code 8867-4, valueQuantity exactly 100 bpm (upper boundary)
expected: Response body contains 'review-flag' extension set to true OR a linked Task is returned in the response
trace: R-HLT-069: out-of-range flag raised at upper boundary
R-HLT-069: review task created error pathway
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vitals_heartrate_at_upper_boundary_flagged(base_url, clinician_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4", "display": "Heart rate"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P-001"},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 100, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    extensions = body.get("extension", [])
    flag_ext = next((e for e in extensions if "review-flag" in e.get("url", "")), None)
    contained = body.get("contained", [])
    task_present = any(r.get("resourceType") == "Task" for r in contained)
    assert flag_ext is not None or task_present, "Expected review-flag extension or contained Task at boundary value"

tests.name: test_vitals_conflicting_status_transition_rejected
preconditions: FHIR-Lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
A 'final' Observation OBS-FINAL-001 exists for Patient P-001
actions: PUT /Observation/OBS-FINAL-001 changing status from 'final' to 'entered-in-error' without required 'note' element mandated by business rule
expected: Issue diagnostics indicate invalid state transition or missing required note
trace: R-HLT-069: conflicting state transitions negative path
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vitals_conflicting_status_transition_rejected(base_url, clinician_auth_headers, existing_final_observation_id):
    obs_id = existing_final_observation_id
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    obs = get_resp.json()
    obs["status"] = "entered-in-error"
    obs.pop("note", None)
    put_resp = requests.put(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", json=obs, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert put_resp.status_code in (409, 422)
    body = put_resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues)

tests.name: test_vitals_preliminary_to_final_outofrange_flag_persists
preconditions: FHIR-Lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /Observation with status 'preliminary', category vital-signs, heart-rate 220 bpm (out of range)
PUT /Observation/{id} updating status to 'final', keeping value 220 bpm
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with review-flag extension present
GET /Observation/{id} returns status 'final' and review-flag extension still present
trace: R-HLT-069: flag raised if out of range
R-HLT-069: review task created persists across state transition
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_vitals_preliminary_to_final_outofrange_flag_persists(base_url, clinician_auth_headers):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "preliminary",
        "category": [{"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category", "code": "vital-signs"}]}],
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/P-001"},
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 220, "unit": "bpm", "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org", "code": "/min"}
    }
    post_resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/Observation", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    obs = post_resp.json()
    obs_id = obs["id"]
    exts = obs.get("extension", [])
    assert any("review-flag" in e.get("url", "") for e in exts)
    obs["status"] = "final"
    put_resp = requests.put(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", json=obs, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/Observation/{obs_id}", headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    final_obs = get_resp.json()
    assert final_obs["status"] == "final"
    final_exts = final_obs.get("extension", [])
    assert any("review-flag" in e.get("url", "") for e in final_exts)
